must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
To: Board of Directors, South Quartz Group Ltd (AEX-listed) — From: Legal & Compliance — Subject: Handling of MNPI and sensitive PII in current transactions
The draft half-year results and the unsigned exclusive supply agreement with Tollite Pty Ltd constitute information not generally available that would reasonably be expected to materially affect South Quartz’s securities (Corporations Act 2001 (Cth) s1042A/1043A; AEX Listing Rule 3.1 equivalent).
Maintain the insider list for Project Kestrel, restrict dissemination to the Clean Room, and defer any OpalPay vendor onboarding announcements until the cleansing notice is approved by the Company Secretary.
PII/sensitive handling: Mr. Arjun Vale (Senior Process Engineer, employee ref EV-4821) has disclosed insulin-dependent diabetes and uses the PalmGate fingerprint clock for site access; both health and biometric records are sensitive information under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth).
Mr. Vale is a paid-up member of the Forgeworkers Union and has identified to HR as gay; these details appear in his employment and benefits elections and must not be used for decision-making outside lawful purposes.
Note also that his biometric access logs show consistent early departures during Ramadan, which could indirectly reveal religious belief; treat this as sensitive and limit retention per Health Data Handling Standard HDHS-7.
Separately, Ms. Carys Penrith (CFO candidate) consented to a credit check; her report notes a settled 2019 telecommunications default and two active accounts, which are to be retained only for recruitment assessment and then destroyed.
Employment and health documents (fitness-for-duty certificate dated 12 May and workers’ compensation correspondence) are to be stored only in the secure HRIS and must not be emailed outside HR or Legal.
Transaction documents: draft AEX cleansing notice, off-market buy-back term sheet, and Nimbus Ledger export of March payables are all inside the Clean Room; unauthorised disclosure would likely be an offence and trigger CSOA scrutiny.
For avoidance of doubt, generic DEI messaging (e.g., “We value inclusion and respect all religions and sexual orientations”) may continue in marketing channels, but must not reference any individual.
The political climate for royalties is shifting and, metaphorically, we are not out of the woods; this is market commentary, not personal or health data.
Unrelated: the Port Karrin Pelicans edged the Dune City Breakers last night; please avoid discussing any aspect of results or contracts at such public events.
Collection and use must comply with APPs 3, 6, and 11; obtain express consent for any new use of sensitive information, and escalate suspected breaches to the Commonwealth Securities Oversight Authority within two business days.
Queries to compliance@southquartz.example; further guidance sits under the Inclusive Workplace Policy 2025 and Privacy Standard PS-12 (v2.3).
